short_term.title: Short-term view (0–30 days · next earnings cycle)
bullets: Run-rate $1.98B in FY2026 Q1, +24.3% YoY — accelerating trajectory entering the next print.
Next-quarter consensus: $0.70B (-64.6% vs current Q) — set by 28 broker contributors.
EPS consensus next qtr: $0.85.
Next earnings: 2026-09-03 — primary catalyst for the short-term window.
short_term.takeaway: Going into the next earnings print, the setup is mixed — execution risk on the next guide is elevated.

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ days · multi-year setup)
bullets: TTM revenue $7B — in cyclical drawdown.
Sell-side: FY1 $3B → FY2 $3B (+22.2% multi-year top-line implied).
Forward EBIT margin: 28.0% — structural premium business over the long-cycle view.
6-quarter run-rate CAGR: +33.9% — the secular trajectory the market is pricing.
Sector thesis (Information Technology): Multi-year AI/cloud capex cycle remains the dominant secular theme.
long_term.takeaway: Long-cycle thesis is intact if the next 2–4 quarters confirm a stabilization in trajectory.

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ days · positioning window)

short_term.title: Short-term view (0–45 DTE · front-month tactical)
bullets: Front-month ATM IV: 115.1% — extreme (binary-event hedging implied).
Put/Call OI: 0.67 — call-heavy positioning (consensus bullish).

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ DTE · LEAPS / structural)
bullets: Back-month avg IV (90+ DTE): 67.1% across 8 expiries.
Available long-dated expiries: 8 dates spanning 91–609 DTE.
90-day implied move: ±34.2% ($119.97 – $244.77).
391-day implied move: ±67.8% ($58.63 – $306.11) — the long-dated 1-σ range.

Term structure in backwardation (-48.0pp front→back) — near-term event premium; long-dated vol cheaper.
long_term.strategy: Sell 6-month strangle: short $164 put / short $201 call. Range-bound + high IV → premium-selling on both sides; profits if spot stays within ±10%; manage with rolls or assignment.
